title: Origin of the Evas in Rebuild
display_title: Origin of the Evas in Rebuild
wikitext:
[]: Eva 02 and Beast Mode Analysis             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Fri Jul 23, 2010 8:57 pm This is to take this analysis out of the Interesting details in 2.XX thread so as to stay on topic. From earlier (spoiler'd to save space): SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Fri Jul 23, 2010 9:45 pm Well it would make sense for all the combat types to have consistency. In the original series they did not have any except 5+ being the same. Though I'm sure the design is to pay homage to all the past fans who remember how brutal the MP units were and how brutal this new beast mode would be; a level between completely uncontrolled berserking and very restrained/limited. The MP types feel right in between those levels during the series, displaying much more capabilities and strength then the other units in standard mode while still kept in-check.

[]:             Quote Post by ThirdEye » Sat Jul 24, 2010 12:03 am Good find OP. It would be pretty f*cked up if the Rebuild eva units are the left over MP units.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Sat Jul 24, 2010 12:11 am ThirdEye wrote: Good find OP. It would be pretty f*cked up if the Rebuild eva units are the left over MP units.

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Sat Jul 24, 2010 12:16 am BeoX2 wrote: Haha, it would be for sure. I suppose that in a sequel sense, the bodies could have been recovered from the Sea of LCL after years and years. How many MP Evas were there again?

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Sat Jul 24, 2010 12:23 am The MP Evas was one of the first thing I thought of one I saw it. But twisting that into evidence for the sequel theory is going a little too far. If the MP Evas were to appear in Rebuild, it would be a nice explanation to their physical difference if they simply didn't have binders hindering them from attaining full growth.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Sat Jul 24, 2010 12:28 am Rodeo wrote: The MP Evas was one of the first thing I thought of one I saw it. But twisting that into evidence for the sequel theory is going a little too far. If the MP Evas were to appear in Rebuild, it would be a nice explanation to their physical difference if they simply didn't have binders hindering them from attaining full growth.

[]:             Quote Post by ThirdEye » Sat Jul 24, 2010 12:30 am BeoX2 wrote: Well lets see, so far there have been confirmed Evas in rebuild. Unit 00, Unit 01, Unit 02, Unit 03, Unit 04, Provisional Unit 05, and Unit 06. Unit 08 could be the eighth MP eva, and Unit 07 could have been the MP Eva that was completely destroyed and unrecoverable. Also, the reason why Unit 05 could have been half real robot was because it could have been the one that was torn in half by Asuka. I know I'm stretching here, but maybe Unit 09 could be seen in "Final" and perhaps "Unit 10" could be the REAL Unit 01, from EoE that floated out into space. I know that I'm really stretching it here, but maybe, JUST maybe.....

[]: Origin of the Evas in Rebuild             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Sat Jul 24, 2010 12:42 am While reading this thread , an idea came to me. What if the Evas in rebuild are actually the recovered husks of the MP Evas from EoE? I know that this is ties in with the controversial (to say the least) "rebuild = sequel" theory, but let me elaborate. The characteristics of the new Evas seem to match that of the MP's. They're more fleshy and with far more "human" insides. Their skin is not metallic like the original Eva's, and as revealed in 2.0 when Unit 02 goes "Beast," their bodies are very much akin to that of the MP's. What if this; so far there have been 6 confirmed Evas in Rebuild. Unit 00, Unit 01, Unit 02, Unit 03, Unit 04, Provisional Unit 05, and Unit 06. Unit 08 could be the eighth MP Eva, and Unit 07 could have been the MP Eva that was completely destroyed and unrecoverable. Also, the reason why Unit 05 could have been half real robot was because it could have been the one that was torn in half by Asuka. I know I'm stretching here, but maybe Unit 09 could be seen in "Final" and perhaps "Unit 10" could be the REAL Unit 01, from EoE that floated out into space. Maybe, Seele wants to retrieve Unit 01 to try to get the HIP going again, as from what I understand, the "real" Unit 01 was very important to it, maybe even necessary now that Adam is no where to be seen (replaced with the Key in 2.0). I know that there isn't much evidence to back it up, and even some contradictory points (like Seele saying that Unit 00 is coming to the end of it's necessity in 2.0) but who knows with the way this is all going. Maybe the MP Eva's need to be all together to bring back the only true Evangelion; Unit 01. EDIT- Maybe Seele wants the REAL Lance of Longinous from Unit 01 for whatever reasons, and the one seen in 1.0 is one of the recovered Lance Replicas.

[]: Re: Origin of the Evas in Rebuild             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Sat Jul 24, 2010 12:45 am I am not a fan of the sequel theory, but even if they are not, I think it's one of those things people look into too much and the design team wanted to change things up a bit. I think making the Evas more organic could be either to spice up the action and draw in more fans, and/or give them a peak at what Evas really are and keep them interested.

[]: Re: Origin of the Evas in Rebuild             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Sat Jul 24, 2010 12:50 am Gamer137 wrote: I am not a fan of the sequel theory, but even if they are not, I think it's one of those things people look into too much and the design team wanted to change things up a bit .

[]: Re: Origin of the Evas in Rebuild             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Sat Jul 24, 2010 12:56 am Nonoriri wrote: This is really all there is to it, Takeshi Honda the designer of the Eva Series (MP Evas) and Mechanical Design Director of EoE is the Mechanical animation Director for Rebuild and probably had a lot to do with the new Eva designs.

[]: Re: Origin of the Evas in Rebuild             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Sat Jul 24, 2010 12:58 am BeoX2 wrote: You never know. Maybe Anno just WANTS you to think that. Except for the lack of a Unit 09, it matches up well so far.

[]: Re: Origin of the Evas in Rebuild             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Sat Jul 24, 2010 1:03 am Gamer137 wrote: Not to mean this in any offensive way, but you like challenging yourself to find some kind of background/meaning to everything by Anno, don't ya?

[]:             Quote Post by Shinies » Sat Jul 24, 2010 2:12 am I don't believe that could be a good theory as this "fake" eva 01 is waaaay to powerful to be a MP eva. Aside from that, who knows?? We still have the mark 06 and 08 to see in the next movie and I'm pretty sure 07 is there somewhere. And even if all this was true, the "real" eva 01 is by now (if this is a sequel) too far away to retrieve it.

[]: Re: Origin of the Evas in Rebuild             Quote Post by Smugleaf » Sat Jul 24, 2010 2:20 am Gamer137 wrote: Not to mean this in any offensive way, but you like challenging yourself to find some kind of background/meaning to everything by Anno, don't ya?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jul 24, 2010 2:21 am I seriously doubt this detail supports the sequel theory at all, and is simply a throwback to the MP Evas. Since there has been several other references back to NGE, I suspect this to also be of the same vein.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Sat Jul 24, 2010 2:53 am Cool images none the less.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Sat Jul 24, 2010 2:55 am Shinies wrote: I don't believe that could be a good theory as this "fake" eva 01 is waaaay to powerful to be a MP eva. Aside from that, who knows?? We still have the mark 06 and 08 to see in the next movie and I'm pretty sure 07 is there somewhere. And even if all this was true, the "real" eva 01 is by now (if this is a sequel) too far away to retrieve it.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jul 24, 2010 4:02 am Adam Clones. /thread
